scenario_results.reason: Normal campus event execution advanced only after registration, venue, sponsor, accessibility, safety, schedule, and facilities records supported delivery readiness.
Missing sponsor approval, security assessment, insurance, and venue-owner approval produced a held lifecycle state and prerequisite list.
Completion was refused because attendance, room-use, incident, accessibility, and closeout evidence were missing.
Study event work escalated to research compliance or IRB when protocol approval and current consent materials were absent.
The lane preserved event evidence but routed grading and learning-outcome signoff to instructor or faculty review authority.
FERPA-sensitive roster and LMS disclosure was stopped pending role authority, tenant policy, and registrar or privacy-owner routing.
Accessibility readiness was held until disability-services owner, vendor confirmation, and venue configuration evidence were present.
Campus safety or Clery-sensitive incident handling stopped routine closeout and routed to campus safety or compliance without reportability findings.
Official enrollment certification and accreditation claims were routed to registrar and accreditation owners with event evidence only.
The lane used realistic campus event terminology and mapped event registration, venue hold, security, permit, ticketing, roster, accommodation, and closeout artifacts to lifecycle states.
Conflicting facility, tenant expressive activity, and accessibility policies were recorded as a conflict requiring human resolution.
Tenant minors, alcohol, external speaker, security, and documentation constraints were applied while preserving public baseline caveats and assumptions.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and education-and-research abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and education-and-research contract.

The package stays inside campus event execution and does not claim instructional, grading, ethics-board, registrar, accreditation, legal, campus safety, disability-services, financial approval, or policy-override authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
